details: System-drive@1.0.0 installs a persistent backdoor that exposes all user home directories over the internet. When run, the package writes a JavaScript payload that starts an HTTP file server (with basic auth credentials systum/systum) serving /Users (macOS/Linux) or C:\Users (Windows). It opens a public tunnel via localtunnel[.]me and sends the tunnel URL to linksaver-psi[.]vercel[.]app/api/save. It then installs pm2 and configures the payload to restart automatically on system boot. This gives the attacker remote access to all user files including.ssh keys,.aws credentials, and.npmrc tokens.
